pdg_node.statement: (signal: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: float) -> tuple[int, int, int, int, int, int, int, int]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Derives all integer/float tuning constants (window sizes, thresholds, buffer lengths, elapsed limits) from the sampling rate and signal duration.

pdg_node.statement: (signal: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: float) -> 1-D float array
pdg_node.informal_desc: Applies a 4th-order Butterworth low-pass filter (25 Hz) followed by a 4th-order Butterworth high-pass filter (3 Hz) to remove baseline wander and high-frequency noise.

declaration.name: bandpass_filter_signal
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: float) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Applies a 4th-order Butterworth low-pass filter (25 Hz) followed by a 4th-order Butterworth high-pass filter (3 Hz) to remove baseline wander and high-frequency noise.

declaration.docstring: Applies a 4th-order Butterworth low-pass filter (25 Hz) followed by a 4th-order Butterworth high-pass filter (3 Hz) to remove baseline wander and high-frequency noise.

declaration.name: bandpass_filter_signal

declaration.docstring: Applies a 4th-order Butterworth low-pass filter (25 Hz) followed by a 4th-order Butterworth high-pass filter (3 Hz) to remove baseline wander and high-frequency noise.

pdg_node.statement: (filtered: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: float, sm_size: int) -> 1-D float array
pdg_node.informal_desc: Computes the absolute first-order derivative of the filtered signal scaled by sampling rate, then smooths it with a Hamming-window kernel to produce a feature signal sensitive to QRS slopes.

declaration.name: compute_smoothed_derivative
declaration.type_signature: (filtered: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: float, sm_size: int) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Computes the absolute first-order derivative of the filtered signal scaled by sampling rate, then smooths it with a Hamming-window kernel to produce a feature signal sensitive to QRS slopes.

declaration.name: compute_smoothed_derivative
declaration.type_signature: (filtered: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: float, sm_size: int) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Computes the absolute first-order derivative of the filtered signal scaled by sampling rate, then smooths it with a Hamming-window kernel to produce a feature signal sensitive to QRS slopes.

declaration.name: compute_smoothed_derivative
declaration.type_signature: (filtered: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: float, sm_size: int) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Computes the absolute first-order derivative of the filtered signal scaled by sampling rate, then smooths it with a Hamming-window kernel to produce a feature signal sensitive to QRS slopes.

pdg_node.statement: (dx: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: float, init_ecg: int, v1s: int) -> tuple[1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D int array]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Segments the first init_ecg seconds of the derivative feature into 1-second windows, finds the maximum peak in each window, and populates the QRS peak amplitude buffer, QRS peak index buffer, and RR-interval buffer used for adaptive thresholding.

declaration.name: initialize_peak_buffers
declaration.type_signature: (dx: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: float, init_ecg: int, v1s: int) -> tuple[1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D int array]
declaration.docstring: Segments the first init_ecg seconds of the derivative feature into 1-second windows, finds the maximum peak in each window, and populates the QRS peak amplitude buffer, QRS peak index buffer, and RR-interval buffer used for adaptive thresholding.

declaration.name: initialize_peak_buffers
declaration.type_signature: (dx: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: float, init_ecg: int, v1s: int) -> tuple[1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D int array]
declaration.docstring: Segments the first init_ecg seconds of the derivative feature into 1-second windows, finds the maximum peak in each window, and populates the QRS peak amplitude buffer, QRS peak index buffer, and RR-interval buffer used for adaptive thresholding.

declaration.name: initialize_peak_buffers
declaration.type_signature: (dx: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: float, init_ecg: int, v1s: int) -> tuple[1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D float array, 1-D int array]
declaration.docstring: Segments the first init_ecg seconds of the derivative feature into 1-second windows, finds the maximum peak in each window, and populates the QRS peak amplitude buffer, QRS peak index buffer, and RR-interval buffer used for adaptive thresholding.

pdg_node.statement: (qrspeakbuffer: 1-D float array, noisepeakbuffer: 1-D float array) -> float
pdg_node.informal_desc: Computes the initial adaptive detection threshold DT as a weighted interpolation between the median noise peak amplitude (ANP) and the median QRS peak amplitude (AQRSP) using the fixed mixing factor 0.475.

declaration.name: compute_initial_detection_threshold
declaration.type_signature: (qrspeakbuffer: 1-D float array, noisepeakbuffer: 1-D float array) -> float
declaration.docstring: Computes the initial adaptive detection threshold DT as a weighted interpolation between the median noise peak amplitude (ANP) and the median QRS peak amplitude (AQRSP) using the fixed mixing factor 0.475.

declaration.name: compute_initial_detection_threshold
declaration.type_signature: (qrspeakbuffer: 1-D float array, noisepeakbuffer: 1-D float array) -> float
declaration.docstring: Computes the initial adaptive detection threshold DT as a weighted interpolation between the median noise peak amplitude (ANP) and the median QRS peak amplitude (AQRSP) using the fixed mixing factor 0.475.

declaration.name: compute_initial_detection_threshold
declaration.type_signature: (qrspeakbuffer: 1-D float array, noisepeakbuffer: 1-D float array) -> float
declaration.docstring: Computes the initial adaptive detection threshold DT as a weighted interpolation between the median noise peak amplitude (ANP) and the median QRS peak amplitude (AQRSP) using the fixed mixing factor 0.475.

pdg_node.statement: (all_peaks: 1-D int array, dx: 1-D float array, signal: 1-D float array, DT: float, qrspeakbuffer: 1-D float array, noisepeakbuffer: 1-D float array, rrinterval: 1-D float array, init_ecg: int, lim: int, diff_nr: int, TH_elapsed: int, v100ms: int) -> 1-D int array
pdg_node.informal_desc: Iterates over all candidate peaks in dx and applies the Hamilton detection rules: (1) skip peaks dominated by a neighbouring peak within 200 ms, (2) require bidirectional slope in the raw signal, (3) reject T-waves when the elapsed time is <360 ms and slope is <50% of previous, (4) classify peaks above DT as QRS or noise, (5) rescue missed beats when no detection occurs within 1.5×RRM, updating the adaptive buffers and RR-interval buffer after every decision.

declaration.name: detect_qrs_candidates
declaration.type_signature: (all_peaks: 1-D int array, dx: 1-D float array, signal: 1-D float array, DT: float, qrspeakbuffer: 1-D float array, noisepeakbuffer: 1-D float array, rrinterval: 1-D float array, init_ecg: int, lim: int, diff_nr: int, TH_elapsed: int, v100ms: int) -> 1-D int array
declaration.docstring: Iterates over all candidate peaks in dx and applies the Hamilton detection rules: (1) skip peaks dominated by a neighbouring peak within 200 ms, (2) require bidirectional slope in the raw signal, (3) reject T-waves when the elapsed time is <360 ms and slope is <50% of previous, (4) classify peaks above DT as QRS or noise, (5) rescue missed beats when no detection occurs within 1.5×RRM, updating the adaptive buffers and RR-interval buffer after every decision.

declaration.name: detect_qrs_candidates
declaration.type_signature: (all_peaks: 1-D int array, dx: 1-D float array, signal: 1-D float array, DT: float, qrspeakbuffer: 1-D float array, noisepeakbuffer: 1-D float array, rrinterval: 1-D float array, init_ecg: int, lim: int, diff_nr: int, TH_elapsed: int, v100ms: int) -> 1-D int array
declaration.docstring: Iterates over all candidate peaks in dx and applies the Hamilton detection rules: (1) skip peaks dominated by a neighbouring peak within 200 ms, (2) require bidirectional slope in the raw signal, (3) reject T-waves when the elapsed time is <360 ms and slope is <50% of previous, (4) classify peaks above DT as QRS or noise, (5) rescue missed beats when no detection occurs within 1.5×RRM, updating the adaptive buffers and RR-interval buffer after every decision.

declaration.name: detect_qrs_candidates
declaration.type_signature: (all_peaks: 1-D int array, dx: 1-D float array, signal: 1-D float array, DT: float, qrspeakbuffer: 1-D float array, noisepeakbuffer: 1-D float array, rrinterval: 1-D float array, init_ecg: int, lim: int, diff_nr: int, TH_elapsed: int, v100ms: int) -> 1-D int array
declaration.docstring: Iterates over all candidate peaks in dx and applies the Hamilton detection rules: (1) skip peaks dominated by a neighbouring peak within 200 ms, (2) require bidirectional slope in the raw signal, (3) reject T-waves when the elapsed time is <360 ms and slope is <50% of previous, (4) classify peaks above DT as QRS or noise, (5) rescue missed beats when no detection occurs within 1.5×RRM, updating the adaptive buffers and RR-interval buffer after every decision.

pdg_node.statement: (beats: 1-D int array, signal: 1-D float array, lim: int, length: int, sampling_rate: float) -> 1-D int array
pdg_node.informal_desc: For each candidate beat index, extracts a ±200 ms window of the original ECG, identifies the dominant positive and negative local peaks, computes their amplitude spreads, and selects the true R-peak location based on which polarity shows greater variability, deduplicating and sorting the final result.

declaration.name: refine_rpeaks_in_raw_signal
declaration.type_signature: (beats: 1-D int array, signal: 1-D float array, lim: int, length: int, sampling_rate: float) -> 1-D int array
declaration.docstring: For each candidate beat index, extracts a ±200 ms window of the original ECG, identifies the dominant positive and negative local peaks, computes their amplitude spreads, and selects the true R-peak location based on which polarity shows greater variability, deduplicating and sorting the final result.

declaration.docstring: For each candidate beat index, extracts a ±200 ms window of the original ECG, identifies the dominant positive and negative local peaks, computes their amplitude spreads, and selects the true R-peak location based on which polarity shows greater variability, deduplicating and sorting the final result.
